title: Problems with dependencies and Lag
excerpt:

posts.author: Vemontoya (Victoria Montoya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All!

I really need some help!

We are working with Monday Dev setting our projects and we ussually use the Strict dependencies.

We notice today that when setting a dependencie linked to a timeline and duration column, the dates on the timeline colum does not reflect the dates are are related to de dependencie and it leaving a lag

and the dates that are shown in the timeline starts with today’s date.

For example:

Sprint 1 depends on Sprint 0, in theory, Sprint 1 should start on Oct 13th but Monday is showing this:

image731×151 14.3 KB

This is happening for all boards and groups we have

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
It sounds like you’re experiencing an issue with how Monday.com’s dependencies are interacting with your timeline and duration columns. When using strict dependencies, the expectation is that the start date of a dependent task should automatically adjust based on the end date of its preceding task. If this is not happening and instead defaulting to today’s date, there could be a few reasons and potential solutions to consider:

  1. Check Dependency Settings: Ensure that the dependency settings are correctly configured. Double-check that the “Strict Dependencies” setting is enabled for your board and that the tasks are correctly linked.

  2. Recalculate Dependencies: Sometimes, recalculating the dependencies can resolve display issues. Try removing and re-adding the dependency to see if it updates correctly.

  3. Column Configuration: Verify that the timeline and duration columns are set up correctly and that there are no conflicting settings or data that might be causing the issue.

  4. Board Settings: Ensure that there are no board-level settings that might be overriding or affecting the dependencies. Check for any automation rules or other settings that could impact how dates are calculated.

  5. Browser Cache: Clear your browser cache or try accessing Monday.com in a different browser to rule out any caching issues that might affect how data is displayed.

  6. Contact Support: If the issue persists after checking the above, it might be a bug or a specific issue with your account. Reach out to Monday.com support with detailed information and screenshots (if possible) to get further assistance.

  7. Feedback to Development Team: If this is a recurring issue that affects your workflow significantly, consider providing feedback to Monday.com’s development team for potential fixes in future updates.

By following these steps, you should be able to identify and potentially resolve the issue with your dependencies not reflecting correctly in the timeline column. If not let me know can try and look into it further!

posts.author: laura.bloom (Laura Bloom)
posts.cookedHtml: Following, I’m experiencing the same thing. It’s intermittent and it doesn’t make a difference when I change web browsers. It does seem to be triggering when I’m adjusting the dependencies/lag time combination. I’ll be opening a support ticket on it.
